highlights.title: Introducing Claude Opus 4.7
highlights.summary: Anthropic has announced Claude Opus 4.7, the latest and most capable model in the Opus line. If you use Claude Code with the default model settings, check your configuration to see if you can upgrade to take advantage of improved reasoning and coding abilities. Visit the Anthropic blog for full details on what's new.

highlights.title: Claude Code v2.1.128 Released
highlights.summary: The latest Claude Code release brings several quality-of-life improvements: typing bare `/color` now picks a random session color, `/mcp` now shows tool counts for connected servers and warns you when a server connects with zero tools, and `--plugin-dir` now accepts `.zip` archives for easier plugin distribution. These small changes make it easier to spot misconfigured MCP servers at a glance.

highlights.title: Claude Code v2.1.126 Released
highlights.summary: Version 2.1.126 adds two useful features: the `/model` picker now lists all models from your gateway's `/v1/models` endpoint when using a custom `ANTHROPIC_BASE_URL`, making it easy to switch models when working behind a compatible gateway. A new `claude project purge [path]` command lets you cleanly delete all Claude Code state for a project — transcripts, tasks, file history, and config entries — which is handy when starting fresh or troubleshooting.

highlights.title: Claude Code v2.1.121 Released
highlights.summary: This release introduces the `alwaysLoad` option for MCP server configs, which forces all tools from that server to always be available rather than deferred by tool-search — useful for servers whose tools you rely on constantly. It also adds `claude plugin prune` to clean up orphaned auto-installed plugin dependencies, keeping your environment tidy. A new type-to-filter feature was also added to make finding items faster.

highlights.title: Full Claude Code Tutorial for Non-Technical Beginners in 2026 (step-by-step)
highlights.summary: This YouTube tutorial is designed specifically for non-technical users who want to get started with Claude Code from scratch in 2026. It walks through setup and basic usage in a step-by-step format, making it a great starting point if you've never used an AI coding assistant before. Bookmark this one to share with teammates or friends who are just getting started.

highlights.title: Mastering Claude Code in 30 minutes
highlights.summary: This YouTube video promises a focused, 30-minute deep dive into Claude Code for developers who want to move beyond the basics quickly. It's a good watch if you've already done initial setup and want to learn more efficient workflows and features without sitting through a lengthy course. Pair it with the latest release notes to get the most up-to-date picture.

highlights.title: Who owns the code Claude Code wrote?
highlights.summary: This Hacker News–linked article explores the legal question of copyright ownership for code generated by Claude Code, a topic that matters for any developer shipping AI-assisted code in a professional or commercial context. Understanding the current legal landscape around AI-generated code can help you make informed decisions about how you use and attribute Claude's output. A worthwhile read before deploying AI-written code in production.

highlights.title: Claude Code v2.1.122 Released
highlights.summary: Version 2.1.122 adds an `ANTHROPIC_BEDROCK_SERVICE_TIER` environment variable so teams using AWS Bedrock can select between `default`, `flex`, and `priority` service tiers directly from their environment config. A handy workflow improvement: pasting a PR URL into the `/resume` search box now automatically finds the session that created that PR, making it easy to pick up where you left off on a feature branch.

tips_of_the_day: Use `claude project purge [path]` to reset all Claude Code state for a project when you hit unexplained bugs or stale context — it's the equivalent of clearing your cache and often fixes mysterious issues fast.

If you use an MCP server whose tools you need on every single task, add `"alwaysLoad": true` to that server's config so its tools are never deferred by tool-search and are always ready without extra prompting.

Before shipping AI-generated code in a commercial product, take 10 minutes to review your company's policy on AI-authored intellectual property — the legal landscape around code ownership is still evolving and varies by jurisdiction.
